trend: Mild bearish for the next 1-3 trading days while NIFTY stays below 24300-24500; acceptance above 24500 would neutralize the bearish read, while loss of 24112/24000 confirms downside.
entry: Direction: sell rallies near 24280-24320 or sell breakdown below 24112; expected probability about 54%; risk-reward about 1:1.4 to target1 and 1:2.2 to target2.
stoploss: Above 24500 on sustained intraday acceptance, or above 24380 for aggressive short entries near 24280-24320
bullish_reasons: NSE live feed shows NIFTY 50 at 24239.15, +32.25 (+0.13%), recovering from the 24000.2 intraday low and trading above max pain 24200.
NIFTY BANK is positive at 58178.3, +132.4 (+0.23%), offering some support to the index.
FII cash flow was positive at about +2604 Cr and DII cash flow was positive at about +2020 Cr as of 2026-07-10.
Put-side positioning remains supportive: NiftyTrader PCR is 1.6881, change-OI PCR is 3.3150, highest Put OI is 24000, and strong Put Writing is visible at 24000/24100/24200.
GIFT NIFTY is mildly positive at 24250.50, +0.21%, though below the +0.5% bullish scoring threshold.
bearish_reasons: FII index futures aggregate positioning is materially net short: 34427 long versus 289138 short, net short 254711 contracts.
India VIX is up sharply at 13.29, +8.44%, so volatility is rising rather than cooling.
PCR at 1.6881 is above the model's 1.4 overheated threshold, so it is scored bearish despite strong put writing.
Fresh call writing is concentrated at 24300, 24200, 24250, 24500, and 24350, keeping the 24300-24500 zone capped unless price breaks out.
NIFTY remains below the heavier 24500 Call OI wall.
US cues are mixed-to-negative because Nasdaq futures and S&P 500 futures are red despite a positive Dow close.
Asian cues are weak overall: Shanghai -2.06% and Nikkei -1.92%, with only Hang Seng slightly positive.
Rupee weakness and higher Brent crude remain macro negatives for India.
NIFTY 50 breadth remains soft at 21 advances versus 27 declines; broader NSE breadth is also negative at 4623 advances versus 4884 declines.
Exact public VWAP and formal CPR bands remain unavailable from accessible text sources; expected range and OI levels were used instead.
alerts: Strong Call Writing: fresh call OI additions are concentrated at 24300, 24200, 24250, 24500, and 24350.
Strong Put Writing: change-OI PCR is 3.3150 and the highest Put OI remains at 24000.
FII Reversal: FII cash is positive, but aggregate FII index futures are heavily net short, creating a bearish institutional divergence.

OI Shift: highest Call OI remains at 24500 and highest Put OI remains at 24000, while near-spot call writing at 24300 caps upside.
Gap Down Recovery: GIFT implies a small negative gap versus NIFTY futures, while spot recovered from 24000.2 but has not cleared 24300/24500.
meaningful_changes: Fresh Strong Call Writing alert: 24300 Calls added the largest OI change, followed by 24200/24250/24500.
Score moved from -5 to -6, which is not a 2-point threshold change; bias remains MILD BEARISH.
Confidence remains 54%, so no confidence-threshold change.
PCR rose from 1.6665 to 1.6881 and change-OI PCR rose from 2.9368 to 3.3150, reinforcing an overheated but put-supported range.
NIFTY remains below 24300-24500 resistance and above 24000 support; no breakout or breakdown confirmed.
